concepts.title: Financial Management is the backbone of business
concepts.description: Financial management is the process of planning, organizing, directing and controlling the financial actions of an organization to achieve its objectives. It is the backbone of business as it deals with the financial aspects of an organization, which is crucial for its survival and growth.

concepts.title: Financial Management is a sub-division of Business Finance
concepts.description: Financial management is a sub-division of business finance that deals specifically with the management of financial resources of a business. It involves planning, organizing, directing and controlling the financial activities of a business to achieve its objectives.

concepts.title: Financial Management is concerned with the maximization of shareholders' wealth
concepts.description: The primary objective of financial management is to maximize the wealth of shareholders by increasing the market value of their shares. This is achieved by making optimal financial decisions that result in maximum returns on investment.

concepts.title: Funding, Investing, Financing, and Divesting (FIFD)
concepts.description: Funding refers to the process of raising funds, Investing refers to the process of investing funds, Financing refers to the process of providing funds, and Divesting refers to the process of disposing of assets. Use the FIFD mnemonic to remember these key concepts in financial management.

concepts.title: Ignoring Time Value of Money (TVM)
concepts.description: Students often ignore the time value of money (TVM) while making financial decisions. This is a common mistake as TVM takes into account the concept of compounding and discounting, which is crucial for making informed financial decisions.

concepts.title: NPV, IRR, Payback Period, and Discounted Payback Period
concepts.description: These four units are crucial for making investment decisions in financial management. NPV (Net Present Value) measures the present value of future cash flows, IRR (Internal Rate of Return) measures the rate at which the investment breaks even, Payback Period measures the time taken to recover the initial investment, and Discounted Payback Period measures the time taken to recover the initial investment after considering the time value of money.

concepts.title: Financial Management is a 'Tool' for achieving business objectives
concepts.description: Financial management is a tool that helps businesses achieve their objectives by making optimal financial decisions. It is not an end in itself but a means to an end.

concepts.title: NPV = ∑(CFt / (1 + r)^t)
concepts.description: The Net Present Value (NPV) formula is used to calculate the present value of future cash flows. It is calculated as the sum of the present value of each cash flow, where CFt is the cash flow at time t and r is the discount rate.
